NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CONTINUED TO MARCH 18, 2008 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF MAR-21-2008 CONTINUED TO MAY-09-2008 AT 9:00 AM IN DEPT. 212. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT REPUBLIC INDEMNITY COMPANY OF AMERICA'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. THE EXEMPTION UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 425.17 IS APPLICABLE TO THIS MOTION. THE GRAVAMEN OF THE COMPLAINT IS THE ALLEGED FAILURE OF DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE THE BENEFITS OF THE INSURANCE POLICY PURCHASED BY THE EMPLOYER AND IS BASED ON THE TERMS OF THE POLICY AND STATEMENTS MADE IN SELLING THE POLICY TO THE EMPLOYER. WHATEVER POSITIONS DEFENDANT TOOK IN THE WORKER'S COMPENSATION PROCEEDINGS ARE INCIDENTAL TO THE THRUST OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. TO ACCEPT DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT WOULD SUBJECT VIRTUALLY EVERY COVERAGE DISPUTE TO AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. C.C.P. SECTION 425.17 REFLECTS THE LEGISLATURE'S INTENT NOT TO DO SO. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Written Responses & Production Of W/Held Documents
Off Calendar May-01-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Terence J. Coleman, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Collen Barger-Carey
Randolph P. Sinnott, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Republic Idemnity Company of America

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Collen Barger-Carey's motion to compel production of documents is granted. The Court finds that the joint client exception to the attorney-client privilege applies and overrules the objection based on relevancy. As to documents that have been identified as work product, if they were not transmitted to the client, said documents need not be produced as they would be subject to protection pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 2018.030(a). Production shall take place within 15 days of notice of this order. Commissioner Bruce Chan. Court Reporter: Judy Manfred, CSR #4748

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plts' Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT REPUBLIC INDEMNITY COMPANY OF AMERICA'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: TERENCE J. COLEMAN, ESQUIRE (PILLSBURY & LEVINSON, 415.433.8000) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. RANDOLPH P. SINNOTT, ESQUIRE (SINNOTT DITO MOURA & PUEBLA, 213.996.4200) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT REPUBLIC INDEMNITY CO. OF AMERICA. RANDY M. MARMOR, ESQUIRE (SINNOTT DITO MOURA & PUEBLA, 415.352.6200) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT REPUBLIC INDEMNITY CO. OF AMERICA.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. THE EXEMPTION UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 425.17 IS APPLICABLE TO THIS MOTION. THE GRAVAMEN OF THE COMPLAINT IS THE ALLEGED FAILURE OF DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE THE BENEFITS OF THE INSURANCE POLICY PURCHASED BY THE EMPLOYER AND IS BASED ON THE TERMS OF THE POLICY AND STATEMENTS MADE IN SELLING THE POLICY TO THE EMPLOYER. WHATEVER POSITIONS DEFENDANT TOOK IN THE WORKER'S COMPENSATION PROCEEDINGS ARE INCIDENTAL TO THE THRUST OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. TO ACCEPT DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT WOULD SUBJECT VIRTUALLY EVERY COVERAGE DISPUTE TO AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. C.C.P. SECTION 425.17 REFLECTS THE LEGISLATURE'S INTENT NOT TO DO SO. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Off Calendar Dec-05-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT REPUBLIC INDEMNITY COMPANY OF AMERICA'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT OF COLLEEN BARGER-CAREY, MICHELLE SHIPLEY AS EXECUTOR OF THE ESTATE OF SHARON J. SHIPLEY AND RALFORD SHIPLEY.
APPEARANCES: Terrence J. Coleman, Esq. of Pillsbury & Levinson, LLP 433-8000 for pltfs & opposing parties; Randy M. Marmor, Esq. of Sinnott, Dito, Moura & Puebla, P.C. 352-6217 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling and adopted as follows: OVERRULED AS TO 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES A BERACH OF THE OBLIGATION TO PAY A WORKER'S COMPENSATION CLAIM AND A BREACH OF THE DUTY TO DEFEND. A DEMURRER MAY ONLY CHALLENGE THE COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED AS TO 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION; 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS TO SUPPORT THE ALLEGED LEGAL CONCLUSIONS. Order signed in open court. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
